(4) If $s\subseteq T$ is totally ordered then it is well ordered. Put 
 
 $E\;=\;\{{\xi}\;|\;\,{s\cap T_{\xi}\neq\varnothing}\}$ 
 and given $\xi\in E$ , let $a_{\xi}$ be the unique element of $s\cap T_{\xi}$ . Define also 
 
 $F\;=\;\{{\eta\in E}\;|\;\,{\min a_{\xi}<\min a_{\eta}\mbox{
whenever }\xi<\eta}\}$ 
 and 
 
 $G\;=\;\{{\eta\in E}\;|\;\,{\max a_{\xi}>\max a_{\eta}\mbox{
whenever }\xi<\eta}\}.$ 
 It is clear that $\{{\min a}\;|\;\,{a\in s}\}$ and $F$ share the same order type, and likewise $\{{\max a}\;|\;\,{a\in s}\}$ has converse order type equal to that of $G$ . Moreover, $E=F\cup G$ . Indeed, if there exist $\xi,\xi^{\prime}<\eta$ such that $\min a_{\xi}=\min a_{\eta}$ and $\max a_{\xi^{\prime}}=\max a_{\eta}$ then, assuming as we can that $\xi\geq\xi^{\prime}$ , we have $a_{\xi}=a_{\eta}$ , which is not allowed in an admissible partition tree. The cardinality assertion follows immediately. ∎

Various efforts have been made to incorporate the two-body resonance between Cooper pairs (open channel) and molecules (close channel) explicitly in the many-body Hamiltonian and many interesting results were obtained. suspicious_text: The intricate art of in-hand manipulation has garnered significant attention, prompting a plethora of methodologies to emerge, spanning from rudimentary feedback control mechanisms to complex trajectory optimization techniques that encompass the entirety of the task. Pioneering research, such as the conceptualization of finger-gaiting and finger-pivoting, relied on simplified modeling to inform the development of control systems. Subsequent advancements have seen the integration of model-based online trajectory optimization, as exemplified by simulations that successfully demonstrate finger-gaiting. Furthermore, recent approaches have combined trajectory optimization with tree search algorithms to enable the precise tracking of a desired object pose trajectory, thereby underscoring the evolving sophistication of robotic manipulation strategies.
source_text: Dexterous in-hand manipulation has long been of interest and numerous methods have been proposed ranging from simple low-level feedback primitive controllers to trajectory optimization for the complete task. Early model-based work on finger-gaiting [6] and finger-pivoting [9] propose controllers using simplified models. [10] use model-based online trajectory optimization and demonstrate finger-gaiting in simulation. [12] combine trajectory optimization and tree search to follow a desired object pose trajectory.

suspicious_text: The PMD method, outlined in Algorithm 1, utilizes the prox-mapping step described in Subsection 3.1 to iteratively update the policy $\pi_{k}$, incorporating the stepsize parameter $\eta_{k}$ and an initial point $\pi_{0}$. For simplicity, this paper assumes an initial policy $\pi_{0}$ with a uniform distribution over all actions, given by $\pi_{0}(a|s)=1/|{\cal A}|$ for all $a$ and $s$. This assumption leads to a bounded divergence $D_{\pi_{0}}^{\pi}(s)$, which can be expressed as $\sum_{a\in{\cal A}}\pi(a|s)\log\pi(a|s)+\log|{\cal A}|$, and is upper-bounded by $\log|{\cal A}|$ for any $\pi(\cdot|s)$ within the simplex $\Delta_{|{\cal A}|}$. Furthermore, it is noted that replacing $Q^{\pi_{k}}(s,\cdot)$ with $A^{\pi_{k}}(s,a)$ in the updating process does not affect the outcome, as this substitution only introduces a constant term into the objective function.

It is established that $\beta \leq 1$ and $\gamma \geq 1$ for all dimensions $d \geq 2$, as demonstrated by Chayes and Chayes [13] and Aizenman and Newman [3]. Additionally, if $\beta$ and $\gamma$ exist, they satisfy $\beta \in (0,1]$ and $\gamma \in [1,\infty)$, as shown in [20]. source_text: Soon after the introduction of the attention mechanism, Transformers based on self-attention mechanism were proposed [9] and have proved to show great power in sequential analysis not only in Natural Language Processing and Audio Processing, but also in Computer Vision. However, many studies show that applying self-attention to long time sequences is still computationally challenging. It takes quadratic complexity of sequential length $L$ in both memory and time. The high complexity is because the self-attention score is computed by taking all the items in the sequence as a squared matrix for each pair of items. To reduce the complexity and utilize the performance of the self-attention mechanism, researchers have proposed some improved Transformer architectures. suspicious_text: In the regime where $x_{0}$ is not much less than 1, the characteristic step-like and plateau features typically seen in the function $M(a_{s}/r_{0})$ become less pronounced in its dependence on $M(w)$. This occurs because the scattering length $a_{s}$ exhibits its weakest dependence on the well depth $w$ precisely in the region where $a_{s}$ is comparable to $r_{0}$, which is where the step-like feature in $M(a_{s}/r_{0})$ is most prominent. Conversely, the plateau regions develop a noticeable slope due to the strong dependence of $a_{s}$ on $w$ near the binding resonance, where $a_{s}$ approaches infinity and the $M(a_{s}/r_{0})$ dependence is weakest. Consequently, the relationship between the quantum defect $\mu$ and the well depth $w$ can be more accurately described as an increasing curve with superimposed modulations, which still originate from the binding properties of the inner potential $U_{s}(r)$.

source_text: The entropy standard deviation bias for finite length encrypted data, denoted as $\sigma_{\alpha}^{bias}$ , can be analysed by simulating the response function of $\sigma_{\alpha}^{bias}$ as a function of number of bits of data. The simulation is based on a set of Monte-Carlo experiments, one for each octet of data. Each standard deviation is the average of an ensemble of 10000 experiments. Bit-length is calculated for each octet as eight times the octet length, in order to have comparable x-axis values for bit- and octet-based data. The experiments are based on simulations using random uniform data selection, which means that a Normal distribution can be assumed.

source_text: In order to better define at what level M 53 is dominated by the FG, we performed simulations of the HB morphology of this cluster. As the HB population is strongly peaked at V $\sim$ 17.0 mag and B–V $\sim$ 0.0, and the HB evolution is strictly redward, there is no possibility of reproducing the sparse blueward extension unless we hypothize that 1) either the mass loss on the RGB is slightly asymmetric; 2) or the bluest HB members have a slightly larger helium content, so that they have a smaller progenitor mass. In this latter case, a small SG population would be present, characterized by this small increase in helium.

This paper is organized as follows: In Section 1.2 , we first introduce simple random walk (SRW) and non-backtracking walk (NBW). In Section 1.4 we state the two basic NoBLE relations that are perturbed versions of relations describing the NBW. Then, we state the results for percolation, lattice trees and lattice animals proved in [17] .

suspicious_text: We introduce a streamlined diagnostic system designed for the detection and characterization of respiratory infections. Leveraging a convolutional neural network (CNN) architecture, this tool effectively identifies cough sounds amidst diverse environmental noises and distinguishes between potential illnesses by analyzing their characteristic acoustic features. The methodology involves transforming cough audio signals into Mel-spectrograms, which serve as the input for both detection and diagnostic processes. Experimental results demonstrate that the CNN achieves high accuracy in performing these tasks, even when trained on a relatively small dataset. Notably, the model's simplicity allows for efficient training with minimal adjustments, enabling its application across multiple diagnostic scenarios.

suspicious_text: The conventional paradigm for analyzing time series data has historically relied on traditional methodologies, supplemented by non-stationary analysis techniques rooted in Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average (ARIMA) models. However, the emergence of Deep Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) has ushered in a new era of time series forecasting, offering a robust framework for capturing complex temporal dynamics [5]. By parsing sequential data, these models are capable of distilling nuanced relationships between temporal events. Notably, Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks [6] and Gated Recurrent Units (GRU) [7] have achieved benchmark performance in numerous time series applications, thanks to the incorporation of forgetting gates that modulate information flow and effectively mitigate the limitations of traditional RNNs, which are often plagued by short-term memory constraints.
source_text: Traditional methods for time series analysis typically involve the classical approaches, and some non-stationary analysis based on ARIMA. The recent advancement of Deep Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) provides new tools for time series forecasting [5] . These models extract the temporal dependencies from time sequences. LSTM [6] and GRU [7] are widely used RNNs that reach the state-of-the-art performance in many time series tasks by introducing forgetting gates that regulate the flow of information, thus overcoming short time memory issues of regular RNNs.

suspicious_text: The bias inherent in the entropy standard deviation of encrypted data with limited length, represented by $\sigma_{\alpha}^{bias}$, can be subjected to scrutiny through an examination of its response function in relation to the quantity of data bits. This analytical approach relies on a series of Monte-Carlo simulations, with each octet of data serving as a basis for an individual experiment. To ensure statistical robustness, each standard deviation value is derived from the mean of 10,000 repeated experiments. Furthermore, to facilitate direct comparison between bit-based and octet-based data, the bit length for each octet is determined by multiplying the octet length by eight, thereby standardizing the x-axis values. Underpinning these experiments is the utilization of random uniform data selection methods, which, in turn, allows for the assumption of a Normal distribution, providing a foundation for the statistical analysis of $\sigma_{\alpha}^{bias}$.
